prediction.reasoning: For a short 3-day period, without specific market news or company catalysts, the most statistically neutral point estimate is the current price, assuming no immediate significant directional movement. The confidence intervals are estimated based on typical historical daily volatility for BRK-B (approximated at 1% daily standard deviation), scaled for a 3-day period using the square root of time rule. This approach assumes price movements follow a normal distribution. Actual price movements can be influenced by broader market trends, company-specific news, or macroeconomic data which are not factored into this model.
